author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
In the Hope of the Resurrection, Let Us Cleave to the Omnipotent and Omniscient God.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

title: In the Hope of the Resurrection, Let Us Cleave to the Omnipotent and Omniscient God.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Having then this hope, let our souls be bound to Him who is faithful in His promises, and just in His judgments.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: He who has commanded us not to lie, shall much more Himself not lie; for nothing is impossible with God, except to lie.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let His faith therefore be stirred up again within us, and let us consider that all things are nigh unto Him.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: By the word of His might He established all things, and by His word He can overthrow them.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: “Who shall say unto Him, What hast thou done? or, Who shall resist the power of His strength?”

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: When, and as He pleases, He will do all things, and none of the things determined by Him shall pass away.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: All things are open before Him, and nothing can be hidden from His counsel.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: “The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament showeth His handy-work.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night showeth knowledge.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: And there are no words or speeches of which the voices are not heard.”
